title: A struct should avoid having member functions
description: A "struct" should not have member functionsAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhy is this an issue?While it is possible to define astructwith member functions in C++, the general expectation is thatstructs only aggregate data, while classes are used for fully encapsulated abstractions, containing data and methods. Thus, including a member function in astructis likely to lead to confusion at best and should be avoided.Noncompliant code examplestruct S { S(int x, int y) : x(x), y(y) {} int x; int y; public: int fun(); // Noncompliant };Compliant solutionstruct S { S(int x, int y) : x(x), y(y) {} int x; int y; };ExceptionsConstructors are ignored for this rule, because they are the only way that astructdesigner can enforce that all fields of astructare correc
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Struct should avoid having member functions (except constructors)
checks.details: Structs should only aggregate data. Use classes for fully encapsulated abstractions with both data and methods.
